clinical.presenting_complaints: Patient presented with a painless reducible swelling in the right groin for the past 8 months. Swelling increases on standing, coughing, and straining and reduces on lying down. Associated dull dragging sensation in the groin. No bowel or urinary complaints. No history of trauma.
clinical.initial_assessment: BP 126/80 mmHg, Pulse 78/min, SpO2 98%, afebrile. Right groin: reducible swelling 4x3 cm, positive impulse on cough, positive deep ring occlusion test. All systemic examination within normal limits. Conscious, cooperative, well-nourished, no pallor or icterus. Weight 68 kg / Height 165 cm | BMI: 24.98. Temp 98.4 F, RR 16/min. CVS S1 S2 heard, no murmurs. Respiratory Air entry bilateral, clear. Abdomen Soft, non-tender, no organomegaly. CNS No focal neurological deficits. Swelling reduces completely in supine position. Left groin: Normal. Scrotum: Normal.
clinical.significant_past_history: No significant past medical history. No chronic illnesses. No known drug allergies. No previous surgeries. Non-smoker, Occasional alcohol.
clinical.primary_diagnosis_at_admission: Right Indirect Inguinal Hernia (Reducible) — ICD-10: K40.9
clinical.final_diagnosis_at_discharge: Post-operative status following successful laparoscopic TEP inguinal hernia repair, right side — ICD-10: K40.9
clinical.key_investigations: Haemoglobin (Hb), WBC Count, Platelet Count, Blood Sugar Fasting, Serum Creatinine, Serum Electrolytes, PT / INR, Chest X-Ray, ECG, USG Abdomen & Groin.
clinical.investigation_findings: All pre-op investigations within normal limits. USG confirms right indirect inguinal hernia with bowel loops in sac. No obstruction or strangulation. Haemoglobin (Hb) 13.8 g/dL, WBC Count 7,200 /cumm, Platelet Count 2.4 Lakh, Blood Sugar Fasting 94 mg/dL, Serum Creatinine 0.8 mg/dL, Serum Electrolytes Na 139, K 4.0 mEq/L, PT / INR 12.4 sec / 1.02, Chest X-Ray No active cardiopulmonary disease, ECG Normal sinus rhythm, USG Abdomen & Groin Right indirect inguinal hernia confirmed. Bowel loops in sac. No obstruction or strangulation. Left side normal.
clinical.treatment_given: Day 1 (19/06/2026): Comfortable post-op, vitals stable, IV fluids continued, oral liquids started. Wound site clean and dry. Day 2 (20/06/2026): Afebrile, soft diet tolerated, mobilising independently, IV antibiotics completed, switched to oral medications. Wound dressing done. Day 3 (21/06/2026): Fully ambulatory, normal diet, wound healing well. Declared medically fit for discharge.
clinical.operative_findings: Indirect inguinal hernia confirmed. Sac ligated and reduced. No strangulation, no visceral injury.
clinical.final_procedure_performed: Laparoscopic Inguinal Hernia Repair — TEP (Totally Extraperitoneal) — Right Side
clinical.advice_on_discharge: Activity: Avoid heavy lifting (>5 kg) for 4 weeks. No strenuous activity for 6 weeks. Warning Signs: Report immediately for fever >100F, wound redness/discharge, swelling at operative site, or urinary difficulty.

Apollo Speciality Hospital
21, Greams Road, Chennai - 600006, Tamil Nadu
Ph: 044-28293333 | Email: records@apollochennai.org
Hospital Reg No: TN/CHE/2003/00412 | PMJAY Empanelment ID: H001
DISCHARGE SUMMARY
Summary No: APH/DS/2026/TCNEW01 Date: 21/06/2026
SECTION 1 — HOSPITAL & PATIENT INFORMATION
Hospital Name Apollo Speciality Hospital
Address 21, Greams Road, Chennai - 600006, Tamil Nadu
PMJAY Empanelment ID H001
Patient Name Murugan Selvaraj
Age / Gender 42 Years / Male
PMJAY Beneficiary ID P001
PMJAY Card No. TN-PMJAY-2024-001234
Address 12, Kamaraj Nagar, Tambaram, Chennai - 600045
SECTION 2 — ADMISSION & DISCHARGE DETAILS
Date & Time of Admission 18/06/2026 at 09:00 hrs
Date & Time of Discharge 21/06/2026 at 11:30 hrs
Total Duration of Stay 3 Days
Type of Admission Planned / Elective
Ward / Room Category General Ward
Treating Consultant Dr. Priya Venkataraman
Qualification MBBS, MS (General Surgery), FMAS
Registration Number TN-MED-20045
Specialty General Surgery
PMJAY Package Booked SG050B — Groin Hernia Repair (Inguinal - Lap.)
Pre-Auth Reference PMJAY-PREAUTH-2026-TCNEW01-001
SECTION 3 — CLINICAL SUMMARY
Presenting Complaints:
Painless reducible right groin swelling for 8 months with dragging sensation. Swelling increased on coughing and
standing, reduced on lying down.
Relevant Medical History:
No significant past medical history. No chronic illnesses. No known drug allergies. No previous surgeries.
Pre-operative Examination:
BP 126/80 mmHg, Pulse 78/min, SpO2 98%, afebrile. Right groin: reducible swelling 4x3 cm, positive impulse on
cough, positive deep ring occlusion test. All systemic examination within normal limits.

SECTION 4 — DIAGNOSIS
Provisional Diagnosis (Admission) Right Indirect Inguinal Hernia (Reducible) — ICD-10: K40.9
Final Diagnosis (Discharge) Post-operative status following successful laparoscopic TEP inguinal hernia repair,
right side — ICD-10: K40.9
SECTION 5 — PROCEDURE PERFORMED
Procedure Name Laparoscopic Inguinal Hernia Repair — TEP (Totally Extraperitoneal) — Right Side
PMJAY Package SG050B — Groin Hernia Repair (Inguinal - Lap.)
Date of Surgery 19/06/2026 at 10:00 hrs
Duration 55 minutes
Anaesthesia General Anaesthesia — Dr. Meena Rajan, MD Anaesthesiology, Reg:
TN-MED-30012
Technique Three-port TEP technique. Preperitoneal space created using balloon dissector.
Polypropylene mesh 6x3 cm placed extraperitoneally covering all groin defects. No
intraoperative complications. Haemostasis secured. Ports closed in layers.
Intraoperative Finding Indirect inguinal hernia confirmed. Sac ligated and reduced. No strangulation, no
visceral injury.
Implant Used Polypropylene Mesh 6x3 cm — Brand: Surgipro, Model: SPM-6030, Serial No:
SRG2026061901
SECTION 6 — POST-OPERATIVE COURSE
Day 1 (19/06/2026): Comfortable post-op, vitals stable, IV fluids continued, oral liquids started. Wound site clean and
dry.
Day 2 (20/06/2026): Afebrile, soft diet tolerated, mobilising independently, IV antibiotics completed, switched to oral
medications. Wound dressing done.
Day 3 (21/06/2026): Fully ambulatory, normal diet, wound healing well. Declared medically fit for discharge.
SECTION 7 — COMPLICATIONS
No intraoperative or post-operative complications.
SECTION 8 — CONDITION AT DISCHARGE
Stable. Afebrile. Pain well controlled on oral analgesics. Surgical wounds clean and dry. Ambulatory without
assistance.
SECTION 9 — DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS
# Medication Dose Frequency Duration
1 Tab. Amoxicillin-Clavulanate 625mg 1 tablet Twice daily 5 Days
2 Tab. Paracetamol 500mg 1-2 tablets As needed 5 Days
3 Tab. Pantoprazole 40mg 1 tablet Once daily 7 Days
4 Tab. Ibuprofen 400mg 1 tablet Twice daily after food 3 Days

SECTION 10 — FOLLOW-UP INSTRUCTIONS
Day 7 (28/06/2026): Wound check and suture removal at OPD.
Day 30 (19/07/2026): Final post-operative assessment.
Activity: Avoid heavy lifting (>5 kg) for 4 weeks. No strenuous activity for 6 weeks.
Warning Signs: Report immediately for fever >100F, wound redness/discharge, swelling at operative site, or urinary
difficulty.
SECTION 11 — DOCUMENTS SUBMITTED WITH THIS DISCHARGE SUMMARY
The following documents are available and submitted along with this discharge summary for PMJAY claim processing:

1 Discharge Summary (CAM Annexure 6 format) Available — this document

SECTION 12 — TREATING DOCTOR'S DECLARATION
I certify that the patient was under my care during this admission, the treatment was medically necessary, the
procedure was performed as documented, and all information in this discharge summary is true and correct to the best
of my knowledge. All mandatory claim documents as per PMJAY/CAM guidelines are enclosed.
